messages.content: \begin{center} \vspace{-5mm}  \begin{tcolorbox}[     colback=gray!3,         % 更淡的背景色，减少视觉压力     colframe=gray!40,        % 边框颜色变淡     arc=1.5mm,               % 稍微圆润一点的圆角     boxrule=0.6pt,           % 细边框更显精致     width=1.0\linewidth,     left=8pt, right=8pt, top=8pt, bottom=8pt,     boxsep=2pt ]  \begin{minipage}[t]{0.30\linewidth}     \centering     \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figs/first_clip.png}      \vspace{1mm}      \includegraphics[width=\linewidth]{figs/second_clip.png} \end{minipage}  \hfill \begin{minipage}[t]{0.69\linewidth}  \tiny \textbf{Question.} Which object is less likely to change shape if you attempted to cut it?  \textbf{Description:} In the first clip, a person with long, dark, matte nails is holding a decorative light bulb. The light bulb made of glass and metal is illuminated with a purple hue and has a holographic effect inside, creating a shimmering appearance. The person rotates the light bulb, showcasing its design and the light it emits. In the second clip, a hand is holding a red, textured object that resembles a piece of candy or a similar material. The hand uses a small knife to cut into the object, revealing its interior which appears to be a smooth, gel-like substance.   \textbf{Thinking:} The light bulb in the first clip is a rigid object made of glass and metal, which would likely shatter or break if cut. The candy-like object in the second clip, however, is flexible and can be cut without changing its overall shape significantly, as it is designed to be cut and consumed. Given this analysis, the object that is least likely to change shape if cut is the candy-like object in the second clip.  \end{minipage}  \end{tcolorbox}  \vspace{-5mm} \end{center} 帮助我排版，右面文字往上提
